methods.description: Collection of flower buds from specific citrus trees (mutant and wild type 'Ougan' mandarin) located in Lishui County, Zhejiang Province, China, at different developmental stages for subsequent observation and analysis.

steps.description: Flower buds were collected from an 8-year-old citrus orchard located in Lishui County, Zhejiang Province, China.
steps.searchable_summary: collect plant samples (flower buds) from a specific geographic location and orchard
inputs: Orchard location (Lishui County, Zhejiang Province, China)

steps.description: The flower buds of the mutant (seedless) were from the clonal multiplication trees top-grafted to Satsuma mandarin as interstock and trifoliate orange [Poncitrus trifoliate (L.) Raf.] as rootstock.
steps.searchable_summary: collect flower buds from mutant citrus trees with specific grafting configuration
inputs: Grafting details (Satsuma mandarin interstock, trifoliate orange rootstock)

steps.description: The wild type (seedy) was grafted on trifoliate orange.
steps.searchable_summary: collect flower buds from wild-type citrus trees with specific grafting configuration

steps.description: Flower buds from three trees both for wild-type and mutant plants of 'Ougan' mandarin were collected
steps.searchable_summary: collect flower buds from a specified number of trees per genotype ('Ougan' mandarin)

steps.description: collected at different stages to observe microspore development from the early premeiotic stage to mature pollen.
steps.searchable_summary: collect flower buds at different developmental stages
inputs: Developmental stages (early premeiotic to mature pollen)

methods.method_name: Pollen Viability and Grain Number Assessment
methods.description: Procedures for assessing pollen viability using two staining methods (iodine-potassium iodide and fluorescein diacetate) and counting the number of pollen grains per anther.

steps.description: Ten flower buds collected from three trees before anthesis were sampled
steps.searchable_summary: collect flower buds before anthesis for pollen analysis

steps.description: all the pollens were freshly harvested.
steps.searchable_summary: harvest pollen from collected flower buds

steps.description: For pollen stain viability, one anther per flower bud was collected from six flower buds, mixed, and spread on 10 slides, and then three observations per slide were completed, including about 100 pollen grains each. Thirty observations per genotype were carried out.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare slides for pollen viability staining (Iodine-Potassium Iodide)
outputs: Observation plan (3 per slide, 100 grains each, 30 per genotype)

steps.description: In the first, the viable pollen grains were stained on the slide with 1% iodine-potassium iodide (KI-I 2 ), and the darkness of the staining was used as an indicator of pollen fertility.
steps.searchable_summary: stain pollen slides with Iodine-Potassium Iodide and assess viability by staining intensity
outputs: Fertility assessment data (based on staining darkness)

steps.description: The second stain test consisted of a drop of fluorescein diacetate (FDA) solution (100 mgÁmL -1 ) was added to the pollen on the slide. After staining for 5 min, pollen grains were observed at WIBA wavelength using a BX61 fluorescence microscope (Olympus, Tokyo). It was assumed that the intense fluorescence is correlated with viable pollen (Liu et al., 2004).
steps.searchable_summary: stain pollen slides with Fluorescein Diacetate (FDA), incubate, and observe fluorescence microscopy for viability

steps.description: To count the number of pollen grain per anther, 100 anthers were collected from at least 10 flower buds, crushed, and mixed in 25 mL of distilled water containing two drops of surfactant (Tween-20, Amresco, Solon, OH).
steps.searchable_summary: prepare pollen suspension from crushed anthers for counting

steps.description: Counts were made in at least on 10 hemocytometer fields to calculate the average number of pollen grains per anther (Chen et al., 2004).
steps.searchable_summary: count pollen grains in hemocytometer fields and calculate average per anther

methods.description: Procedure for fixing, dehydrating, embedding, sectioning, and staining flower buds for cytological observation using light microscopy. Includes a specific method for squashing and staining anthers.

steps.description: Both the wild-type and the mutant flower buds were fixed overnight in 5 formalin : 5 acetic acid : 90 alcohol [FAA (by volume)] at 4°C.
steps.searchable_summary: fix plant tissue (flower buds) using FAA solution at 4°C overnight

steps.description: Fixed flower buds were dehydrated using a graded ethanol series (50%, 70%, 95%, and 100%)
steps.searchable_summary: dehydrate fixed plant tissue using a graded ethanol series

steps.description: and embedded in Technovit 7100 resin (Heraeus, Kulzer, Germany).
steps.searchable_summary: embed dehydrated plant tissue in resin

steps.description: Transverse sections %1 mm thick were cut from the embedded blocks using a Leica Ultracut R ultramicrotome (Leica, Bensheim, Germany).
steps.searchable_summary: cut transverse sections from embedded tissue blocks using a microtome

steps.description: The sections were stained with 2% Toluidine Blue O (Aldrich, Milwaukee) solution for cytological observation.
steps.searchable_summary: stain tissue sections with Toluidine Blue O for cytological observation

steps.description: Several anthers fixed in FAA were squashed and stained with 1% acetocarmine in 45% acetic acid to observe microsporocyte morphology.
steps.searchable_summary: squash FAA-fixed anthers and stain with acetocarmine for microsporocyte morphology observation

steps.description: All observations were performed under a light BX-61 fluorescence microscope.
steps.searchable_summary: observe stained sections and squashes using a light microscope

methods.method_name: Histochemical Localization of Polysaccharides, Lipids, and Callose
methods.description: Specific staining procedures applied to anther sections to detect and localize insoluble polysaccharides, lipids, and callose using light microscopy.

steps.description: To detect insoluble polysaccharides, anther sections were oxidized for 10 min in 0.5% periodic acid in 0.3% nitric acid and rinsed in running water for 1-2 min, with a final rinse in distilled water. After staining for 15 min in Schiff's reagent, the sections were washed three times in 0.5% sodium metabisulfite for 2 min each, rinsed for 5 min in running water, and transferred to distilled water.
steps.searchable_summary: stain anther sections for insoluble polysaccharides using Periodic Acid-Schiff (PAS) method

steps.description: To detect lipids, anther sections were rinsed for 1-2 min in 70% ethanol, stained in fresh 1% Sudan Black B in 70% ethanol for 30-60 min at 50-60°C, rinsed for 1 min in 70% ethanol, and transferred to distilled water (Xie et al., 2005).
steps.searchable_summary: stain anther sections for lipids using Sudan Black B

steps.description: For callose, sections were stained with 0.01% (w/v) Aniline Blue in 0.1 M phosphate buffer, pH 9 (Zhang and Fernando, 2005).
steps.searchable_summary: stain anther sections for callose using Aniline Blue
inputs: 0.01% Aniline Blue in 0.1 M phosphate buffer (pH 9)

steps.description: All observations were performed under a light BX-61 fluorescence microscope.
steps.searchable_summary: observe stained sections using a light/fluorescence microscope

steps.description: All photographs were taken using an Olympus DP 70 CCD camera.
steps.searchable_summary: capture images of microscopic observations

methods.method_name: Scanning Electron Microscopy (SEM) of Mature Pollen
methods.description: Procedure for preparing FAA-fixed anthers containing mature pollen for observation using scanning electron microscopy to study pollen grain morphology.

steps.description: The FAA-fixed anthers were dehydrated using a graded ethanol series (50%, 70%, and 95%)
steps.searchable_summary: dehydrate FAA-fixed anthers using a graded ethanol series

steps.description: and degreased by treating them twice, 20 min each, with xylene.
steps.searchable_summary: degrease dehydrated anthers using xylene

steps.description: This was followed by critical-point drying,
steps.searchable_summary: perform critical-point drying on degreased anthers

steps.description: mounting on copper stubs,
steps.searchable_summary: mount dried samples on SEM stubs

steps.description: and sputter coating with gold.
steps.searchable_summary: sputter coat mounted samples with gold

steps.description: The samples were examined under a Quanta 200 scanning electron microscope (FEI Co., Hillsboro, OR),
steps.searchable_summary: examine gold-coated samples using a scanning electron microscope (SEM)

steps.description: and representative images were obtained.
steps.searchable_summary: obtain representative images from SEM examination
